01.00.title: Home page
01.00.body:
The Home page is the landing page of the Telco Home app which opens automatically once the user is logged in. 
At the top of the home page is an AI generated City/State Image of the user’s approximate neighborhood. This is set by the user’s location, and is not customizable.
If the user has more than one address associated with their account, in the top-left corner of the header, there will be the name or address of the location they are currently controlling. Selecting this dropdown will allow the user to control a different address associated with their account. The user will have a different set of equipment for each location. In the top-right corner of the header, there is a button called Notifications, when tapped takes the user to see all their notifications.
Beneath the AI generated image is a section called “Connections”. There are 3 tiles within Connections: Equipment, Network, and Devices. Each tile has a dot which shows connection health, along with a label that says “Online” or “Offline” if the equipment, network, or devices are online or offline. Green dot means online, healthy, amber dot means unhealthy connection, grey dot means offline.
If the user selects the Equipment tile, it will take them to the Equipment page (02.00). If they select the Network tile, it will take them to the Network management page (03.00). If they select the Device tile, it will navigate them to the All devices page (04.00).
Beneath the Connections section is the “Quick actions” section. This section has 4 buttons, each linking to a different feature of the App: Share Wi-Fi (when selected, takes the user to the Share Wi-Fi page (03.06)), Troubleshoot (when selected, takes the user to the Troubleshoot page (01.01)), Speed Test (when selected, takes the user to the Router Speed Test page (01.02)), and Restart Router (when selected, takes the user to the Restart Router page (02.07)). 
Beneath the “Quick actions” section is a section called “Digital Secure Home”. In this section, there is an interactable tile that, if the user has not ever enrolled in Digital Secure Home service, describes the functionality of Digital Secure Home, along with a CTA called “Enroll for $0/mo”, when selected, takes the user to the enrollment flow for Digital Secure Home, where they can follow the on-screen instructions to enroll.
If the user has subscribed to any tier of Digital Secure Home, but has already cancelled their subscription to Digital Secure Home, this tile will be removed from this page, and will instead show on the Discover page (05.00).
If the user has subscribed to any tier of Digital Secure Home, and the subscription is still active, then this section will contain 3 interactable elements. The first is a Dashboard button, when selected, will take the user to the Digital Secure Home page (12.04). The second is a tile called “Threats blocked”, that includes an indicator of the number of threats blocked in the past 7 days, when selected, will take the user to the Threats blocked page (12.05). The third is a tile called “Vulnerabilities found”, that includes an indicator of the number of vulnerabilities found as well as the last scan date and time, when selected, will take the user to the Vulnerability Assessment page (12.06).
Beneath the “Digital Secure Home” section is a section called “Parental Controls” (note that if the user has cancelled their Parental Controls subscription, the “Parental Controls" section will not be on the homepage”). In this section, there is an interactable tile that describes the functionality of Parental Controls, along with a CTA called “Enroll for $0/mo”, when selected, takes the user to the Enrollment page (13.01).
Once the enrollment process for Parental Controls is complete, a new button will appear called “All profiles” (when selected, will take the user to the Parental Controls page (13.00)), and the CTA in “Parental Controls” tile will change to “Create a Profile”, when selected, will take the user to the Create profile page (13.02).
If the user has enrolled in Parental Controls, and has at least one profile already created, then the “Parental Controls” tile will no longer be shown. Instead, an interactable tile for each of the already created profiles will be shown in its own profile tile. Each profile tile will have a photo of the profile, and the name of that profile, when selected, will take the user to the Profile detail page (13.03) for that profile. The user can scroll left or right to see all of their created profile tiles here. The last tile in this section (the right-most tile) will always be an Add profile tile, when selected, will take the user to the Create profile page (13.02).
Beneath the “Parental Controls” section, is a “For you” section, which shows other Telco product offerings. 
The Bottom Navigation bar has three tabs: Home, Discover, and More. The Home tab will navigate the user to this page, the Home page (01.00). The Discover tab will take the user to the Discover page (05.00). The More tab will take the user to the More page (06.00).
Troubleshoot Page

01.01.title: Troubleshoot page
steps: Confirm the extender is powered — the status LED should be solid white. Amber or blinking usually means it has lost its backhaul to the main router.
From the Home page, tap Equipment → select the extender → tap "Restart extender".
Wait 60 seconds for the extender to rejoin the mesh. The LED should return to solid white.
01.01.body:
This is the Troubleshoot page. At the top of the page it says “Guided troubleshooting”. Below that is a list of 7 guided troubleshooting flows which can help the user solve various network problems. If the user selects on any option, it will bring them to its respective troubleshooting flow. The troubleshooting flows are as follows, in order from top to bottom: 
Internet connection issues 
Slow internet speed
Router, device and browser settings
All FiOS services out
Batter beeping issues
Repair status
Equipment or service line issues
Router Speed Test page

01.02.title: Router speed test page
steps: Select the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
Select the "Router speed test" option towards the top of the Router Details page.
Select "Start speed test".
01.02.body:
The Router Speed Test page allows users to check their router’s internet speed. Note that this is NOT the same as a device speed test, which tests internet speed on individual devices such as phones, gaming devices, smart devices, televisions, etc. There is no device speed test function in this app. This test will help users to see if their routers are having connectivity issues, or if they are operating and connecting properly. 
There are two tabs at the top of the Router Speed Test page: Speed Test, and History.
Speed Test:
This tab shows a large button which says “Start: Test speed between your router and the internet”. Once selected, it will show an animation as the router automatically tests its connection speed. From here how many devices are actively connected to it.
On completion, the test will display a set of results including download speed, upload speed, and latency.
History:
The History tab shows a list of all the speed tests the user has run, and their results.
Discover Page

02.00.title: Equipment page
02.00.body:
The Equipment page is the page that lists all the equipment that the user has connected to the network.
At the top of the Equipment page is a list of the user’s equipment. This list of equipment shown is in a particular order, and is dependent on if the user is a Telco Fios customer, Telco 5G Home Internet customer, and if the user also has Telco Fios TV service or not. Each item on the list is an interactive tile that always shows the equipment image, the equipment name, the equipment location/home name, and the equipment status. If the user selects on any of these tiles, it will bring them to the associated Equipment details page (ONT details page (02.01), Router details page (02.02), Gateway details page (02.03), Extender details page (02.04), Receiver details page (02.05), Set-Top Box details page (02.06)). 
The equipment location for an ONT is the location where the ONT is physically installed. The equipment location/home name for a router or Gateway can be edited in the Router details page (02.02) or the Gateway details page (02.03), depending on the user’s equipment. The equipment location for any Fios TV equipment is the room that it is placed in, and can only be edited on the Set-Top Box’s onscreen TV Menu.
The equipment status can be “Online” in green, “Weak Signal” in orange, or “Offline” in black. The ONT status can only be “Online” or “Offline”.
If the user is a Telco Fios customer, the first item on the list is the ONT, labeled “Optical Network Terminal (ONT)”, when selected, takes the user to the ONT details page (02.01). The second item on the list is the router, when selected, takes the user to the Router details page (02.02). If the user also has any extenders, they will be listed after the router, and when selected, takes the user to the Extender details page (02.04), the user can have multiple extenders.
If the user is a Telco 5G Home Internet customer, the first item on the list is the Gateway, when selected, takes the user to the Gateway details page (02.03). If the user has a receiver, it will be listed after the Gateway, when selected, takes the user to the Receiver details page (02.05). If the user has any extenders, they will be listed after the receiver.
If the user also has Fios TV equipment, then there will be two lists on this page, each list in its own accordion UI component that can be expanded or collapsed. The first list will be labeled “Network”, and in the following order, could contain ONTs, routers or Gateways, extenders, and receivers. The second list will be labeled “Fios TV”, and in the following order, could contain the primary set-top box, then the satellite set-top boxes. Selecting any of the Set-top Boxes will bring the user to the Set-Top Box Details Page (02.06).
In the Fios TV list, the primary set-top box will always be listed before any satellite set-top boxes. 
If the user does not have any Fios TV equipment, then there will only be a list of network equipment that the user has, and is not in an accordion UI component.
ONT Details Page

02.01.title: ONT details page
02.01.body:
The ONT details page is the page where users can see their ONT details.
At the top of the ONT details page, is a section that includes the following basic details about this specific equipment: an image of this specific equipment, the equipment status (which can be “Online” in green, or “Offline” in black), the name of this specific equipment, a tooltip button, and the model number for this specific equipment.
Beneath the basic details section is a section called Technical information, which includes information about this specific equipment in the following order: Model number, Serial number, WAN Connectivity, ONT State, Ethernet Port State and Install Date.
Equipment Details Page

02.02.title: Equipment details page
02.02.body:
**This page can take multiple forms: Router Details, Gateway Details, or Extender Details. Depending on what the user’s query is, respond with the word “Equipment” replaced with either, “Router”, “Gateway”, or “Extender”. Certain sections of this page will only be available for certain kinds of equipment. These will be noted in their headers.**
Only provide this deep link for Routers and Gateways.
Associated Deep Link: [Equipment details](telcohome://equipment-details)
Level 1
For Extenders, show these steps to reach Extender Details:
Accessible from: Equipment Page (02.00)
Level 2
Router / Gateway / Extender:
The Equipment details page is the page where users can manage their Equipment details.
At the top of the Equipment details page, is a section that includes the following basic details about this specific equipment: an image of this specific equipment, the equipment status (which can be “Online” in green, “Weak Signal” in orange, or “Offline” in black), the name of this specific equipment, a tooltip button, and the model number for this specific equipment.
Selecting the equipment name or the tooltip button will open a small tooltip window that contains a brief explanation of this specific equipment’s functionality. Selecting the equipment name or the tooltip will not change the current page. Selecting the equipment name or the tooltip while the tooltip window is open will close the tooltip window.
Beneath the basic details section is a list of the actions the user can take to manage this specific equipment. The list of actions is shown in tiles (each tile also has a brief description of its functionality) in the following order: “Home name”, “Equipment Speed Test”, “Restart Equipment”, “WPS (Wi-Fi Protected Setup)”, “Save settings”, “Reset or restore settings”, “LED brightness”.
Router / Gateway ONLY:
In the Home name tile, it shows your current custom name for this specific equipment, and an Edit button to the right of this tile, when selected, takes the user to the Edit your home name page (02.15)
Selecting the Equipment Speed Test tile takes the user to the Equipment Speed Test page (01.02).
Selecting the Restart Equipment tile takes the user to the Restart Equipment page (02.07).
Selecting the WPS (Wi-Fi Protected Setup) takes the user to the WPS page (02.08).
Selecting the Save settings tile takes the user to the Save settings page (02.08).
Selecting the Reset or restore settings tile takes the user to Restore or reset page (02.09).
Extender ONLY:
In the location tile, it shows your current custom location for this specific equipment, and an Edit button to the right of this tile, when selected, takes the user to the Edit location page (02.12).
Router / Gateway / Extender :
In the LED brightness tile, there is an indicator that shows the current LED brightness of this specific equipment, when selected, takes the user to the LED brightness page (02.10), where they can change the brightness of their equipment’s LED.
Extender ONLY:
When the Remove extender tile is selected, it will initiate the process to remove this specific extender from the user’s network, and the user will have to set it up again to use this specific extender. 
Router / Gateway / Extender:
Beneath the list of actions is a section called Technical information, which includes information about this specific equipment in the following order: Model number, Firmware version, Serial number, IPv4 address, IPv6 address, and MAC address.
Receiver Details Page

02.05.title: Receiver details page
02.05.body:
The Receiver details page is the page where users can manage their Receiver.
At the top of the Receiver details page, is a section that includes the following basic details about this specific equipment: an image of this specific equipment, the equipment status (which can be “Online” in green, “Weak Signal” in orange, or “Offline” in black), the name of this specific equipment, and a tooltip button.
Selecting the equipment name or the tooltip button will open a small tooltip window that contains a brief explanation of this specific equipment’s functionality. Selecting the equipment name or the tooltip will not change the current page. Selecting the equipment name or the tooltip while the tooltip window is open will close the tooltip window.
Beneath the header section is a tile for Restart receiver, when selected, takes the user to the Restart receiver page (02.14).
Beneath the list of actions is a section called Technical information, which includes information about this specific equipment in the following order: Model number, Make, Firmware version, and Serial number.
Set-Top Box Details Page

02.06.title: Set-top box details page
02.06.body:
The Set-top box details page is the page where users can manage their set-top box.
At the top of the Set-top box details page, is a section that includes the following basic details about this specific equipment: an image of this specific equipment, the equipment status (which can be “Online” in green, “Weak Signal” in orange, or “Offline” in black), the name of this specific equipment, and a tooltip button.
Selecting the equipment name or the tooltip button will open a small tooltip window that contains a brief explanation of this specific equipment’s functionality. Selecting the equipment name or the tooltip will not change the current page. Selecting the equipment name or the tooltip while the tooltip window is open will close the tooltip window.
Beneath the basic details section is a list of the actions the user can take to manage this specific equipment. The list of actions is shown in tiles (each tile also has a brief description of its functionality) in the following order: “Remote Control” (note that this list item will only be shown if the user has Fios TV One or Fios TV+), “Remote Control Pairing Instructions” (this list item will only be shown if the user has Fios TV One or Fios TV+), “Restart equipment” (Note that for this list item, the work “equipment” will be replaced by the name of the user’s equipment, for example: if the user has a Fios TV One, it will say “Restart Fios TV One”), and “Troubleshoot” (note that this list item will only shown if the user has a Fios TV+).
In the “Remote Control” tile, the battery level for the remote control will be shown (If the remote control battery will be a different color depending on its battery level: 31%-100%, the percentage level will be green. 1%-30%, the percentage level will be orange. (N/A) 0%, the Find My Remote button will be disabled). Next to the battery level will be a button called “Find My Remote” (note that this button will only be shown if the user has Fios TV One with a Voice Remote, or Fios TV+ with a Fios TV+ Remote). Selecting the “Find My Remote” button will initiate the Find My Remote process, which will trigger the user’s remote to begin audibly pinging to help users locate their remote (note that the “Find my Remote” button will be disabled for 10 seconds after pressing it, the user will need to wait 10 seconds before trying to ping their remote again).
Selecting the “Remote Control Pairing Instructions” tile will show a popup with information for how to pair their remote control to their set-top box. The instructions will be different depending on if the user has a Fios TV One/Fios TV One Mini or a Fios TV+.
Selecting the “Restart set-top box” tile will take the user to the “Restart set-top box page (02.13).
Selecting the “Troubleshoot” tile will take the user to the Telco Fios TV+ Support webpage.
Beneath the list of actions is a section called Technical information, which includes information about this specific equipment in the following order: Model number, Serial Number, Unit Address, IP Address, and Connection Type.
Restart Router Page

02.07.title: Restart Router page
aliases: Turn off and on again router
turn the router off and on
steps: Select the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
Select the "Restart router" option towards the top of the Router Details page.
Select "Restart router".
02.07.body:
The Restart Router page is the page where users can follow the on-screen instructions to restart their router or gateway. Restarting the router or gateway may help with connectivity issues.
At the top of the Restart Router page, is the page header that says “Restart Router”. If the user has a gateway, then it will say “Restart gateway” instead.
Beneath the header section is an image of your current router or gateway. 
Beneath the image of your equipment, there is a button called “Restart Router”, or if the user has a gateway, the button will say “Restart gateway” instead. If the user has done a restart previously, above the button will also show the time of the previous restart. When the user selects this button, the on-screen instructions will instruct the user to begin a router or gateway restart.
The restart process could take up to 10 minutes, and most app functionality will be disabled until the restart is successfully completed.
If the restart fails for any reason, there will also be a Troubleshooting button at the top of the page, when selected, takes the user to the Troubleshoot page (note that this button will only show if the Restart Router process fails).
WPS Page

02.08.title: WPS page
steps: Select the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
Select the "WPS (Wi-Fi protected setup)" option towards the top of the Router Details page.
If WPS is not enabled, enable WPS using the toggle towards the top-right of the page.
Select the "Start WPS" button at the bottom of the page.
02.08.body:
Deep Link: [WPS](telcohome://equipment-wps)
Level 1
The WPS page is the page where users can follow the on-screen instructions to start WPS for this equipment. Starting WPS (Wi-Fi Protected Setup) initiates a secure Wi-Fi connection to your devices without using a password.
To use WPS, the user’s broadcast Wi-Fi name needs to be enabled at mynetworksettings.com (otherwise WPS will be disabled). The user needs to find their admin password on the router or gateway label.
If the user’s network is currently using WPA3 encryption, then it is not compatible with WPS. The user needs to change their encryption setting to WPA2 or WPA2/WPA3 transition mode to use WPS.
At the top of the WPS Page, is the header section. On the left side of the header section is a Back button, when selected, should take them to the previous page. At the center of the header section is the page header that says “WPS”.
Beneath the header section is a section called “Connect devices via WPS”, this section includes a description that says “Select the Start WPS button on this screen, then, within 2 minutes, press the WPS button on the device you want to connect.” The WPS button will be a physical one on the device or a virtual one in the device’s software.
Beneath the “Connect devices via WPS” section is a toggle button labeled “WPS (Wi-Fi Protected Setup)”, that when toggled, will toggle WPS on or off.
Beneath the WPS toggle is an image of the current equipment. 
Beneath the image is a “Start WPS” button, when selected, will start WPS, and while it is establishing a connection, it will take around 2 minutes for it to complete the process.
If the connection is successful, a button will appear that says “Connect another device”.
If the connection times out, please try again, as it failed to connect a device within the 2 minutes.
Save Settings Page

02.09.title: Save settings page
steps: Select the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
Select the "Save settings" option on the Router Details page.
Select the "Manual save" button.
02.09.body:
The Save settings page is the page where users can automatically or manually save and backup their settings for this specific equipment. The user can save their custom router or gateway settings including Wi-Fi names, passwords and more.
At the top of the Save settings page, is the header section. On the left side of the header section is a Back button, when selected, should take them to the previous page. At the center of the header section is the page header that says “Save settings”. 
Beneath the header section will show the “Last save” section, which shows the previous time the user has saved their router or gateway settings. 
Beneath the “Last save” section, the user can toggle on or off automatic settings saving, which will automatically save the user’s router or gateway settings every 7 days. Beneath this is a “Manual save” button, which will manually save the user’s settings whether automatic save is on or off. The user’s back up will be saved for 14 days and timestamped by default.
Restore or Reset Page

02.10.title: Restore or reset page
steps: Open Equipment and select the router or gateway.
Open Restore or reset.
Choose Restore from backup or the appropriate reset option.
Confirm the restore or reset action.
02.10.body:
The Restore or reset page is the page where users can restore their router/gateway settings from a backup, and factory reset their router/gateway. 
At the top of the Restore or reset page, is the header section. On the left side of the header section is a Back button, when selected, should take them to the previous page. At the center of the header section is the page header that says “Restore or reset”. 
Beneath the header section are 3 checkbox options, only one can be selected at any one time. The 3 options are in the following order: “Restore from backup”, “Factory reset, but keep credentials”, and “Factory reset all settings.”
“Restore from backup” will restore the user’s router/gateway settings to a backup that was saved earlier. The user must also select from a dropdown of previously saved backup settings. Restoring saved settings will replace the user’s current settings, this process will also restart the router/gateway, and may take up to 3 minutes. This option will only be available if the user has a previously saved backup settings.
“Factory reset, but keep credentials” will reset the router/gateway but keeps the user’s current network names and passwords, this process will also restart the router/gateway, and may take up to 5 minutes.
“Factory reset all settings” will erase all of your router/gateway settings, this process will also restart the router/gateway, and may take up to 5 minutes.
Beneath the 3 options is a Continue button, when selected, will initiate with the process according to which of the 3 options the user has checked. Below the Continue button is a Cancel button, which when selected, will take the user to the previous page. 
If any of the 3 processes fails, the user will need to try again.
Don’t suggest a Reset unless it is either the only solution to a problem, or if the user specifically requests it.
LED Brightness Page

02.11.title: LED brightness page
steps: Select the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
Select the "LED brightness" option on the Router Details page.
Choose which brightness level you would like your Router to have.
Select "Save" at the bottom of the page.
02.11.body:
The LED brightness page is the page where users can adjust the LED brightness and timing for this specific equipment. 
At the top of the LED brightness page, is the header section. On the left side of the header section is a Back button, when selected, should take them to the previous page. At the center of the header section is the page header that says “LED brightness”. 
Beneath the header section is an image of this specific equipment.
Beneath the image are 3 checkbox options in the following order: “0%”, “50%”, and “100%”.
Beneath the 3 options is a “Cancel” button, which when selected will take the user to the previous page. Next to the Cancel button is a “Save” button, which when selected will save the current selected checkbox option for LED brightness.
Note that the light will always activate on status changes like WPS pairing or loss of connection.
Note that changing LED brightness settings may take up to 5 minutes to reflect on the equipment.
Note that specifically for the equipment called “Wi-Fi Extender Mini”, the LED brightness settings will match and be managed by the router itself. For the Wi-Fi Extender Mini, this page will have a link to the router’s LED brightness setting.
Edit Location Page

02.12.title: Edit location page
steps: Open Equipment and select the extender.
Open Edit location.
Choose a listed location or enter a custom location name.
Save the location change.
02.12.body:
The Edit location page is the page where users can manage this specific extender’s location.
At the top of the Edit location page, is the header section. On the left side of the header section is a Back button, when selected, should take them to the previous page. At the center of the header section is the page header that says “Edit location”.
Beneath the header section, the user can select from a dropdown of locations. The user can also input a custom location name.
At the bottom of the page are 2 buttons: “Save”, which when selected will save the currently selected location from the dropdown menu for this specific extender. The second button is a “Cancel” button, when selected, will take the user to the previous page.
Restart Set-Top Box Page

02.13.title: Restart set-top box page
02.13.body:
The Restart Set-top box page is the page where users can follow the on-screen instructions to restart their set-top box. Restarting the set-top box may help with connectivity issues.
At the top of the Restart set-top box page, is the header section. On the left side of the header section is a Back button, when selected, should take them to the previous page. At the center of the header section is the page header that says “Restart set-top box” (note that the word “Set-top box” will be replaced with the user’s equipment name, for example: if the user has a Fios TV One, then the header will say “Restart Fios TV One”). 
Beneath the header section is an image of your current set-top box equipment. 
Beneath the image of your equipment, there is a button called “Restart set-top box” (note that the word “set-top box” will be replaced with the user’s equipment name, for example: if the user has a Fios TV One, then the header will say “Restart Fios TV One”). If the user has done a restart previously, above the button will also show the time of the previous restart. When the user selects this button, the on-screen instructions will instruct the user to begin a set-top box restart.
The restart process could take up to 10 minutes, and most Fios TV functionality will be disabled until the restart is successfully completed.
If the restart fails for any reason, there will also be a Troubleshooting button at the top of the page, when selected, takes the user to the Troubleshoot page (note that this button will only show if the Restart Router process fails). 
Restart Receiver Page

02.14.title: Restart receiver page
02.14.body:
The Restart receiver page is the page where users can follow the on-screen instructions to restart their receiver. Restarting the receiver may help with connectivity issues.
At the top of the Restart receiver page, is the header section. On the left side of the header section is a Back button, when selected, should take them to the previous page. At the center of the header section is the page header that says “Restart receiver”. 
Beneath the header section is an image of your current receiver equipment. 
Beneath the image of your equipment, there is a button called “Restart receiver”. If the user has done a restart previously, above the button will also show the time of the previous restart. When the user selects this button, the on-screen instructions will instruct the user to begin a receiver restart.
The restart process could take up to 10 minutes, and most receiver functionality will be disabled until the restart is successfully completed.
If the restart fails for any reason, there will also be a Troubleshooting button at the top of the page, when selected, takes the user to the Troubleshoot page (note that this button will only show if the Restart Router process fails). 
Edit Your Home Name Page

02.15.title: Edit your home name page
steps: Open Equipment and select the router or gateway.
Open Edit your home name.
Enter the new home name.
Save the home-name change.
02.15.body:
The Edit your home name page is the page where users can manage this specific router or gateway’s home name.
At the top of the Edit your home name page, is the header section. On the left side of the header section is a Back button, when selected, should take them to the previous page. At the center of the header section is the page header that says “Edit your home name”.
Beneath the header section is a text input field where the user can type in their desired home name.
At the bottom of the page are 2 buttons: “Save”, which when selected will save the currently inputted home name. The second button is a “Cancel” button, when selected, will take the user to the previous page.
Equipment Troubleshooting
If the user is experiencing issues with their Telco Equipment:
First, verify that the equipment is plugged in and receiving power.
Second, power cycle their equipment by unplugging it, waiting thirty seconds, then plugging it back in.
Third, ensure that their equipment is unobstructed.
Extender speed vs. coverage:
Extenders do not necessarily boost speed, they only boost coverage. This may help improve connection for devices that are far from the user’s router or gateway, but will not necessarily boost speed if coverage is already good.
Several factors, from the physical environment to the hardware and software they’re using, may affect their Wi-Fi:
Distance from the router
The farther they are from the router, the weaker the signal becomes.
Physical barriers
Walls, floors and other solid objects can significantly block or weaken their Wi-Fi signal.
Channel overcrowding
In crowded areas, like apartment buildings or offices, multiple Wi-Fi networks may be competing for the same channels, causing congestion and slowing down their connection.
Computer components
Components within their computer, including processor speed, amount of available memory, operating system and computer configuration variables, can affect their Wi-Fi signal.
Router placement
Avoid placing the Router in corners, on the floor or near large appliances.
Non-Wi-Fi devices
Many household electronics operate on the same frequencies as Wi-Fi, including microwave ovens, cordless phones, Bluetooth devices, baby monitors and some smart home gadgets.
Electrical devices
Electrical devices that generate electromagnetic fields, such as fluorescent lights and motors, can also disrupt Wi-Fi signals.
Network outages
Unusual events like a network outage and failure may also affect their Wi-Fi performance. Sometimes the internet sites they try to access may have issues on their end related to outages, server failure or network congestion.

03.00.title: Network Landing Page
03.00.body:
This is the Network Page. Do not refer to this as the Network Landing Page. It is the central location for Network Management and Optimization tools.
At the top of the page is an image of the user’s Telco equipment. It shows the status, Online or Offline, and has a link to the Equipment Page. 
Beneath the equipment header, are two quick action buttons: Share Wi-Fi, which takes the user to the Share Wi-Fi Page (03.02), and Speed Test, which takes the user to the Router Speed Test Page (01.02).
Beneath the quick actions is the Wi-Fi Management tile. This tile takes the user to the Wi-Fi Management Page (03.04).
Beneath the Wi-Fi Management tile is the Wi-Fi Performance tile. It shows a summary of the user’s current Wi-Fi Performance: Weak, Fair, Good, Excellent. This tile takes the user to the Wi-Fi Performance Page (03.05).
Beneath the Wi-Fi Performance tile is the Advanced Tools tile. This tile takes the user to the Advanced tools page (03.10).
At the bottom of the page is the Featured Tools section. This section highlights any new Network Tools in the app. 
The Featured Tools section contains the Wi-Fi Signal Analyzer tile, which takes the user to the Wi-Fi Optimizer Page (03.11)
Wi-Fi Management Page

03.01.title: Edit Wi-Fi page
aliases: change wifi security to wpa3 mixed mode
steps: Open Network and choose the Wi-Fi network.
Tap Edit Wi-Fi.
Update the Wi-Fi name, security mode, or password.
Save changes.
03.01.body:
This is the Edit Wi-Fi page. On this page users can edit the details of their Wi-Fi network.
From top to bottom:
Wi-Fi Name
Security (WPA2, WPA3, WPA2/WPA3 Mixed mode)
Wi-Fi Password (As the user creates their password, this also shows password security strength)
At the bottom of the page is a button called “Save Changes”, which will save any edits the user has made.
Share Wi-Fi Page

03.02.title: Share Wi-Fi page
steps: From the Home page, tap "Share Wi-Fi" under Quick actions.
Choose the network you'd like to share (main or guest).
Show the QR code on your phone — the guest scans it with their camera and joins automatically, no password typing.
03.02.body:
This is the Share Wi-Fi page. There are three tabs on this page: Primary, Guest, and IoT.
Primary tab:
This tab includes the Name and Password of the user’s primary Wi-Fi. 
Below that information are two buttons: “Share” and “Edit Wi-Fi details”. The “Share” button will allow the user to send your network information through any messaging app. The “Edit Wi-Fi details” button will bring the user to the Edit Wi-Fi page (03.01).
Below this section is a QR code which can be scanned to Share Wi-Fi details automatically.
Guest: 
This tab includes the Name and Password of the user’s guest Wi-Fi. 
Below that information are two buttons: “Share” and “Edit Wi-Fi details”. The “Share” button will allow the user to send your network information through any messaging app. The “Edit Wi-Fi details” button will bring the user to the Edit Wi-Fi page (03.01).
Below this section is a QR code which can be scanned to Share Wi-Fi details automatically.
IoT: 
This tab includes the Name and Password of the user’s IoT Wi-Fi. 
Below that information are two buttons: “Share” and “Edit Wi-Fi details”. The “Share” button will allow the user to send your network information through any messaging app. The “Edit Wi-Fi details” button will bring the user to the Edit Wi-Fi page (03.01).
Below this section is a QR code which can be scanned to Share Wi-Fi details automatically.
SON Page

03.03.title: SON page
03.03.body:
SON stands for Self-Organizing Network. With SON enabled, the user’s devices will automatically connect to the optimal Wi-Fi network/band while keeping all primary bands under one Wi-Fi name and password.
With SON disabled, the user is required to choose among available Wi-Fi networks with varying speeds and bandwidths (2.4 GHz, 5 GHz, 6 GHz) depending on their router or gateway. Each network on each piece of equipment would need to be managed separately, and each would require their own Wi-Fi name and password.
This page has a toggle to enable or disable SON.
All devices Page

03.04.title: Wi-Fi Management Page
steps: Open Network.
Choose Wi-Fi Management.
Select Primary, Guest, or IoT.
Use the network toggle to turn that Wi-Fi network on or off.
03.04.body:
At the top of the page, there are 3 tabs: Primary, Guest, and IoT.
Primary:
The Primary network is the user’s main network. It is displayed on a tile with a header which displays the Network Name, and a toggle to turn it on and off. On that tile, beneath its header, is the Wi-Fi password, which is hidden by default. The user can select the “Show” button to temporarily reveal the Wi-Fi password.
Beneath that, on the tile, are two buttons: “Edit Wi-Fi” and “Share Wi-Fi”. The “Edit Wi-Fi” button will bring users the Edit Wi-Fi Page (03.01). The “Share Wi-Fi” button will bring the user to the Share Wi-Fi Page (03.02).
Beneath the Primary network tile is a toggle to turn on a 6 GHz band. The 6 GHz Wi-Fi band can offer faster connection for compatible devices. Note: enabling the 6 GHz band will change the user’s Network Security Type to WPA3. Older internet-enabled devices may not be able to connect through this connection type. To fix this issue, users should go to their Security section within Edit Wi-Fi, and change it to WPA3/WPA2 Mixed mode.
Beneath that is the Self-Organizing Network button. It will either display as Enabled, or Disabled. Selecting on this button will bring the user to the SON Page (03.03).
Beneath that is the Tools section.
The Troubleshoot tile takes the user to the Troubleshoot page (01.01).
The Reset Wi-Fi tiles takes the user to the Reset Wi-Fi page (03.08).
The Restart Router tile takes the user to the Restart Router Page (02.07).
The Data Usage History tile takes the user to the Data Usage History Page (03.07).
The Network Map tile takes the user to the Network Map Page (04.01).
Guest:
This section displays details for the user’s Guest network. This is useful for keeping their primary network free of clutter, so that their device list isn’t populated with devices that the user doesn’t need to manage. It is displayed on a tile with a header which displays the Network Name, and a toggle to turn it on and off. On that tile, beneath its header, is the Wi-Fi password, which is hidden by default. The user can select the “Show” button to temporarily reveal the Wi-Fi password.
Beneath that, on the tile, are two buttons: “Edit Wi-Fi” and “Share Wi-Fi”. The “Edit Wi-Fi” button will bring users the Edit Wi-Fi page (03.01). The “Share Wi-Fi” button will bring the user to the Share Wi-Fi page (03.02).
IoT:
This section displayed details for the user’s IoT network. IoT stands for “Internet of Things”. This is a dedicated 2.4 GHz network for smart devices and appliances. It is displayed on a tile with a header which displays the Network Name, and a toggle to turn it on and off. On that tile, beneath its header, is the Wi-Fi password, which is hidden by default. The user can select the “Show” button to temporarily reveal the Wi-Fi password.
Beneath that, on the tile, are two buttons: “Edit Wi-Fi” and “Share Wi-Fi”. The “Edit Wi-Fi” button will bring users the Edit Wi-Fi Page (03.01). The “Share Wi-Fi” button will bring the user to the Share Wi-Fi Page (03.02).
Wi-Fi Performance Page

03.05.title: Wi-Fi Performance Page
03.05.body:
This page updates hourly. At the top of the page are two tabs: Wi-Fi health, and Device health.
Wi-Fi health:
At the top of the Wi-Fi health section is a summary of the user’s Wi-Fi coverage, speed, and reliability, measured as: Weak, Fair, Good, and Excellent. 
If the user’s Wi-Fi Performance score is noted to be less than Good, the page will list Recommended actions which the user can take to improve their Wi-Fi performance. These actions include: 
Turn of Self-Organizing Network (SON)
Turn on 6GHz band
Repair Wi-Fi
Restart Router
Reset Wi-Fi
Each of these recommendation tiles will lead the user to the appropriate flow to complete these actions. 
At the bottom of the page is the Explore section.
Explore section:
Within the Explore section is the Advanced Tools tile, which takes users to the Advanced Tools Page (03.10).
Device Health:
At the top of the Device health section is a chart which represents the connection quality of all of the devices connected to the user’s Home Internet. Device health shows you how well an individual device, like your smart TV or laptop, is connecting to your network.
Each device's health is given a status of excellent, good, fair or weak which is measured by their:

Signal strength is the quality of the Wi-Fi signal the device is receiving.
Data rate is the speed at which data is being sent and received by the device.
If a device has a weak or fair status, it might be in a location with a poor signal or have an issue that's affecting its performance. Note: Occasionally you’ll notice a calculating status. This means we're recalculating your rating to give you the most up-to-date information.
Beneath the chart is a list of all connected devices, along with their connection health.
Advanced Tools Page

03.06.title: Device Priority Page
03.06.body:
This feature is only available on some Telco Routers and Gateways. Not all customers may have access to this feature.
At the top of the page is the Device Priority toggle, which allows the user to toggle the feature on and off.
With Device Priority on, the user can improve the connection speed of up to 2 devices. These devices will remain prioritized until the user deprioritizes them. This could impact the connection speed of other devices on their network.
The user may need to refresh any apps or websites they’re using to activate your improved speeds.
The user must press and hold on a device from their device list on their All Devices Page (04.00) to activate device priority.
Data Usage History Page

03.07.title: Data Usage History Page
03.07.body:
Accessible from  Network Landing Page (03.00) > Wi-Fi Management Page (03.04)
Level 3
This feature is only available on some Telco Routers and Gateways. Not all customers may have access to this feature.
Data Usage History tracks the user’s network usage by time and gigabytes. Here, the user can see how much time or data they’ve spent on different categories of services: 
Streaming
Social media
Gaming
Shopping
Messaging
Conferencing
Finance
Education
News
Software
Miscellaneous
The user can also view usage history of specific devices, and narrow down usage to certain days.
Reset Wi-Fi Page

03.08.title: Reset Wi-Fi Page
03.08.body:
This feature returns changed Wi-Fi settings to their default. Your current Wi-Fi network name (SSID) and password won’t be affected. This may help the user to optimize device connections, by refreshing their connection to the Wi-Fi.
During this process, your devices may be temporarily disconnected from Wi-Fi, but will reconnect once it completes.
At the bottom of the page, is the Reset now button, which will trigger this process. This will take under 1 minute.
Latency Analyzer

03.09.title: Latency Analyzer
03.09.body:
This feature allows users to see their connection latency to web services like apps, websites, and games.
Sometimes connection issues may not be due to issues with the user’s home internet, and may be in their connection to other services.
At the top of this page is a graph which shows the Latency and Jitter of the user’s connection to their Router or Gateway. Latency is measured in milliseconds, and represented in the Stability column as: Weak, Good, and Excellent.
Jitter measures the spikes in latency over time, as: Weak, Good, and Excellent.
Beneath this graph, is a list of services, along with their latency and connection strength. At the top of the list is a button “Add Service”, which will allow the user to add a new service to this list for a maximum of 14 services.
The user may select and hold on any service to bring up a menu, allowing them to move that service to the top of the list, or delete it.
Wi-Fi Optimizer

03.10.title: Advanced Tools Page
03.10.body:
This page contains a set of tiles connecting users to various Advanced Network Tools throughout the app.
The Troubleshoot tile takes the user to the Troubleshoot page (01.01).
The Reset Wi-Fi tile takes the user to the Reset Wi-Fi page (03.08).
The Restart Router tile takes the user to the Restart Router page (02.07).
The Wi-Fi Signal Analyzer tile takes the user to the Wi-Fi Signal Analyzer page (03.11).
The Device Priority Tile takes the user to the Device Priority page (03.06).
The Latency Analyzer tile takes the user to the Web Services Check page (03.09).
Edit Wi-Fi Page

03.11.title: Wi-Fi Optimizer
aliases: where should i place wifi extender
03.11.body:
Wi-Fi Optimizer lets users analyze their Wi-Fi in different rooms around their home, and assist the user in identifying areas of poor Wi-Fi coverage around their home, make informed decisions on where to place your devices for the most stable and reliable connection and suggest helpful Wi-Fi tips to improve their Wi-Fi strength.
Users can follow the on-screen instructions and select from a list of rooms available or a custom room, the size of the room, and then test for Wi-Fi signal strength, throughput and latency.

04.00.title: All devices page
04.00.body:
The Devices page allows the user to manage each device connected to their home network. At the top of the Devices page, within the header, there are three buttons, Back, Search, and Info. The Back button will return the user to the Home page (01.00). The Search button allows the users to search through the devices connected to their home network. The Info button answers frequently asked questions about the Devices page.
Below the header is a button called Network Map. Selecting on this button will bring the user to the Network Map page (04.01). 
Below the Network Map button, is the Device List header. This header displays the number of devices currently connected to the user’s home network. To the right of the header, there are two buttons: Filter, and Sort. 
If the user selects on the Filter button, it will bring up a modal which will allow the user to filter their device list by: Device type, Connection type (Wired, Wireless), Frequency band (5 GHz, 2.4 GHz), and Device status (Online, Offline, Weak signal, Paused).
If the user selects on the Sort button, it will bring up a modal which will allow the user to choose how their device list is sorted: Device name A-Z, Device name Z-A, Date added newest to oldest, or Date added oldest to newest.
Below the Device List header is the Device List itself. It is made up of Device cells, which contain basic information about each device connected. One cell for one device. Each Device cell contains, on the left, an icon representing the device type, the device name, the kind of device it is (ex: Mac, Smart TV, Soundbar). On the right of the device cell is the connection state (Online, Offline, Weak signal, Paused), and an arrow icon, which indicates that the cell is clickable.
The user can press and hold on a device from their device list to activate Device Priority (03.06).
If a user selects on a Device cell, it will bring them to that device’s Device Details Page (04.02).
Network Map Page

04.01.title: Network Map page
04.01.body:
The Network Map offers customers a visual representation, a diagram, of their home network. At the top of the Network Map page, within the header, there are two buttons, Back, and Info.
Beneath the header, the Network Map displays the customer’s primary Wi-Fi Access Point, their router or gateway, on the leftmost side of the screen. Branching off of their primary piece of equipment are lines which visualize how each device on their network is connected. Each line represents whether the device is connected through a 2.4 GHz band, a 5GHz band, or a wired connection. Each device is represented with an icon representing what type of device it is (computer, smart tv, phone, etc), along with the device name. Each device has a dot which represents their connection strength (Strong, Weak, Disconnected). 
If the customer has Telco Extenders, these will also be shown on the map, along with their connection type and strength like a device. If there are any devices connected to the network through these extenders, they will be represented by a numbered badge, indicating how many devices are connected through that extender. To the right of the badge, there will be an arrow which, if pressed, allows the user to expand the map, showing the details of each of the devices connected to that extender, in the same way as devices connected to the router.
On the Bottom Right corner of the page, there are two buttons: Filter, and Refresh. 
If the user selects the Filter button, a modal will pop up with filtering options: Wired, Wireless, 2.4GHz, 5GHz, 6GHz, Online, and Offline. By default, the Online filter is applied on the map, hiding offline devices.
If the user selects the refresh button, the map will reload, to show any connection updates.
Device Details Page

04.02.title: Device details page
04.02.body:
This is the Device details page, which displays information for each device connected to your home network. Each device details page is different for each device connected, so a user will have a different device details page for every device connected to their network. For example, if a user has 8 devices connected to their network, they will have 8 individual device details pages.
At the top of the page, as a title section, is the connection status of a device (Online, Offline, Weak Signal, Paused). Just below that is the device’s name. Below that is the device type (Computer, Tablet, etc). 
Below the title section is a list of device controls, and information. At the top of this list is the device’s connection information. It displays how the device is connected to the user’s home internet: Wired, 2.4 GHz, 5 GHz, or 6GHz. It also displays the Telco equipment that the device is connected through, and its name (it could be through their Router/Gateway, or through an extender.)
Below the connection information is a pause internet button. Pause Internet allows the user to temporarily block a device from accessing their home network. 
Below Pause Internet, is Profiles. They allow parents to group devices together into Profiles for each of their children to control their internet access on all their devices at once. If a device is not assigned to a Profile, the Device Details Page will show Profile, Unassigned. If the user selects on this, the user will be prompted to add the device to an existing profile, or create a new profile. Selecting Create a New Profile will bring users to the Create Profile Page (13.02). If the device is assigned to a Profile, instead this cell will show the profile name, and Selecting it will bring the user to the Profile Page (13.03).
Beneath Profiles, is the Device Nickname. It displays: Nickname: [Device Name]. On the far right of the cell, there is an option to Edit. Selecting Edit will prompt the user to edit the Device Nickname.
Beneath the Device Nickname is the Device Type, which shows what kind of device this is (Tablet, Computer, Console, etc).
Beneath the Device type is the Location. By default the location of a device is unassigned, but if the user selects the edit button on the right side of the cell, they can add the device to a specific room or location in their home to better organize their home network.
Beneath Location is Troubleshoot device. If the user selects the Troubleshoot device cell, it will pull up a modal with some quick internet fix tips. At the bottom of the modal is a button: More troubleshooting. Selecting this button will bring the user to the Troubleshoot Page (01.01).
Below Troubleshoot device is a Technical Information section. At the top of Technical Information, under the title, “Technical Information”, is a link called “Learn more about technical details”. This link brings up a modal that provides definitions for each of the technical terms in this section. Below the link are two sections: Device Info, and Network Info. 
The Device Info section contains: Connection type, RSSI, Phy/Modulation, SNR, Hostname, IPv4 address, IPv6 address, and MAC address.
The Network Info section contains: Network Name, Band, Protocol, and Encryption.
Device Priority Page

05.00.title: Discover page
05.00.body:
The pricing information for Wi-Fi Backup can be found here (https://www.telco.com/home/internet/wi-fi-backup/).
The Wifi Backup will only work during a power outage if it has a battery backup or if your home uses a generator.
If a user uses all the seven sessions, they will have to wait till the next billing cycle to get the next seven sessions. A user cannot add additional sessionsUsers are limited to seven sessions per billing cycle. Once these sessions are exhausted, users must wait until the next billing cycle to access additional sessions; it is not possible to add more sessions within the current cycle. Once started, a Wi-Fi backup session will run its full time of 24 hours, you cannot end or roll-over remaining time on a Wi-Fi backup session. Users can extend their session by another 24 hours during an ongoing session.
If wifi backup is not working or offline, it could be either the user has ran out of sessions or they need to reposition their gateway. If the wifi backup is not maintaining a connection, then user needs to reposition their gateway.  If none of these solve the problem, [Contact us] (tel://+18008374966).
Common Wi-Fi backup issues include:“My WiFi backup router has a red blinking light and won't connect to the internet"
Solution: Unplug the Wifi Backup Gateway, wait 10 seconds and plug it back in. Then wait up to 10 minutes until the system status light stops blinking white, and try again. If the blinking red light persists after a restart, contact  [Contact us] (tel://+18008374966).
"The WiFi signal light keeps flashing red on my backup device”
Solution: No Wi-Fi Backup session is active. Start a session in the app to connect.
“My WiFi backup has been stuck in the same state for multiple days and cannot connect"
Solution:
Ask the user to confirm what state it is in (what the lights are showing).
If the status light is solid red, it means the Wi-Fi Backup Gateway can't find a signal. Try a different setup spot – near windows or perimeter walls tend to get the strongest signal.
If the status light is blinking red, unplug the Wi-Fi Backup Gateway, wait 10 seconds and plug it back in. Then wait up to 10 minutes until the system status light stops blinking white, and try again. If the blinking red light persists after a restart, [Contact us] (tel://+18008374966).
If the status light is blinking white, it means a firmware update in progress.
If the Wi-Fi light is blinking red, it means no Wi-Fi Backup session is active. Start a session in the app to connect.
“I cannot activate my WiFi backup router through the app - it keeps failing during activation”
Solution: Unplug the Wifi Backup Gateway, wait 10 seconds and plug it back in. Then wait up to 10 minutes until the system status light stops blinking white, and try again. If the issue persists,  [Contact us] (tel://+18008374966). 
Wifi backup is not a regular home internet service. It is a backup internet to your primary internet service. For regular home internet service, Telco has Fios or 5G home internet available based on the user's zip code.Unlike standard home internet, Wifi Backup serves as a secondary internet service for your primary connection. For typical home internet solutions, Telco offers Fios or 5G Home Internet, depending on your zip code.
Parental Controls
Parental Controls Overview
Parental Controls is a Telco service that provides control over a user’s child’s online activities on router-connected devices. 
Currently users can enroll in Parental Controls subscription for $0/month, and this is the only tier of Parental Controls in the Telco Home app.
Note that Parental Controls and all of its sub-features and pages are only available for Fios equipment (and not available for any FWA equipment), and if the user has already enrolled in Parental Controls. 
Note that there is a similar feature in My Telco app called HNP, and if that is enabled, it may conflict with this Parental Controls feature.
Note that there is a similar feature in Telco Family, for more information on this, go to: https://www.telco.com/support/residential/internet/essentials/fios-digital-secure-home-plus-faqs/.
Parental Controls page

06.00.title: More page
06.00.body:
The more page is the central hub for account settings, billing information , and managing biometric login (like Face ID). You can also report and track issues directly from this page. Additionally, this page provides access to the Telco Community forums, important legal and consumer information, and details about the app's version number and release notes.
At the top left of the More page is a button called Account Settings (06.01), when selected, takes the user to the account settings page.
To the right of the Account Settings (06.01) button, is a menu item called My Telco app (10.00). Selecting on this takes the user outside the home to My Telco app. If the user doesn't have My Telco app, it takes the user to either Android playstore or iOS app store depending on which OS they have on their mobile and prompts the user to download the my telco app. My Telco app allows the user to pay their bills, manage their plan, check out the latest offers and more.
Beneath the Account settings button, is a toggle button called Face ID (for Android, this button is called Biometric Login), when selected, will toggle to enable or disable Face ID as biometric login.
To the right of the Face ID button is a button called Legal, and when selected, will take the user to the Legal page (06.09).
Below the 4 above buttons, is a section called Support. The support section has 4 buttons. The first button, on the top left of this section, is a button called Help & Feedback, when selected takes the user to the Help & Feedback page (06.04). The second button is a button called Support inbox, when selected, takes the user to the Support inbox page (06.05), where the user can see messages, and close issues. The third button, on the bottom left of this section, is a button called Contact us, when selected, takes the user to a page with the following options: Chat with us,. The fourth button, on the bottom right of this section, is a button called Telco Community Forums (06.07). Selecting on this takes the user to Telco Community forums where users can view all publicly answered questions.
Beneath the Support section is the version number of the app. 
At the bottom of the page, we see a Sign Out button. Selecting it signs out the user from the home app.
Account Settings Page

06.01.title: Account Settings Page
06.01.body:
At the top of the Account settings page (06.01), we can see the user’s account number. Beneath the user’s account number is a section called Profile. Under the Profile section we see User ID and Contact info menu items.
Selecting on the User ID menu item under the Profile section allows the user to rename their ID. After entering a new ID, a save button becomes active at the bottom. Selecting Save updates the user’s ID.
Selecting on the Contact info menu item under the Profile section allows the user to edit their account greeting name, account email address, the mobile/phone number associated with their account. After entering a new greeting name and/or email address and/or mobile number. Beneath the mobile number is a check box toggle with which the user can opt in or out of text message notifications. The save changes button at the bottom of the page updates the user’s entered account information.
Under the contact info menu item is a section called Security & authentication. Under the security & authentication section we see password, secret question and account pin menu items.
Selecting the password menu item under the Security & authentication section allows the user to change their password. To change the password, the user needs to type in their current password, followed by New Password and confirm the new password. Once the user enters these details, the save button becomes active at the bottom. Selecting Save updates the user’s password.
Selecting the secret question menu item under the Security & authentication section allows the user to answer specific security questions. The user can select a predefined security question from the drop down and enter the answer below. Once the user enters the answer, the save becomes active at the bottom. Selecting Save updates the security question and answer.
Selecting the Account pin menu item under the Security & authentication section allows the user to set up/manage/edit their 4-digit account PIN (pin/PIN). 
Help & feedback

06.04.title: Help & feedback
steps: Open More.
Open Help & feedback.
Choose the relevant issue or feedback option.
Submit the report or feedback.
06.04.body:
On the Help & feedback page, which allows users to report an issue or enter any feedback about the app (note that if users has login issues, do not suggest them to come to this page, instead they should: [Call us] (tel://+18009220204).
Examples of issues include:
App not working or not displaying any information.
Equipment or device not displaying, or showing offline but Wi-Fi or network is working.
Equipment or equipment information not detected.
Device or device information not detected.
App Stability:
The app crashing immediately after you open it, even after restarting your phone.
The app freezing on the loading screen or becoming unresponsive after you enter a password or PIN.
“Something went wrong:
Restart the app.
Power cycle (reboot) the router.
If the issue persists, the user should report the issue by creating a ticket through the "Help & feedback" page of the app.
It was advised not to direct the user to "Contact Us" (customer service phone number) for this scenario, as the support team reached by phone would not be able to do much to help.
“Something went wrong” error immediately:
The primary recommendation is to completely close out of the app, then reopen.
If that fails, the user will not be able to access the in-app "Help & feedback" feature to report the issue.
In a scenario if the user is not able to access the help & feedback page due to the app not responding or freezing, the user must go to the My Telco app (MVA) or telco.com to report the issue.
Feature-Specific Bugs:
The app incorrectly showing your router as "offline" even though your internet is working perfectly (note that in this case, the user can try to fix this by first making sure that they are not using any third-party equipment, then restarting the app, making sure the app is up to date, and restarting their router, as these could be possible fixes).
A severe delay (e.g., more than a minute) between pressing "pause" in Parental Controls and the internet actually stopping for that device.
The "Assign Profile" option in Parental Controls being greyed out when you have fewer than 16 devices assigned.
General Performance Issues:
The app is consistently slow, laggy, or clunky during normal use, making it difficult to navigate (note that users may try to fix this by restarting the app, making sure the app is updated, and restarting their router, but do not suggest a speed test).
Formal Complaints and Feedback:
A formal complaint about a negative experience with a customer service agent or support channel.
Reporting a potential service outage in your area after you've already confirmed your own equipment is working correctly.
Once the user enters the information the Submit button becomes active at the bottom, when selected, sends the issue or feedback to the Telco Home App team. 
Just below the submit button is a Cancel text link button that allows the user to go back to More page (06.00).
New Category: When You Are Unable to Report an Issue:
This category covers critical failures where the app itself prevents you from reaching the Help & Support page.
Examples:
The app crashes instantly upon opening, making it impossible to navigate to any menu.
The "Help & Support" button is unresponsive, frozen, or leads to an error page.
Solution:
For critical failures within the Telco Home app, an alternative reporting channel is available through the My Telco app's support page.
Telco Home Device Protect
Telco Home Device Protect
Telco Home Device Protect is a monthly subscription service that offers protection, repair, replacement, and technical support for a wide array of eligible home electronics and smart devices. It also includes benefits like in-home expert visits and installation services.
2. Eligibility
Must be a Telco customer with either:a. A Telco broadband account (Fios Home Internet, 5G Home Internet, or LTE Home Internet).b. A Telco mobile account with an eligible plan (e.g., Unlimited plans, certain shared data plans).
Cancellation of Telco mobile or home service will result in the termination of Telco Home Device Protect.
The plan does not cover devices already covered by Telco mobile device protection options (like Telco Mobile Protect).
3. Cost
Monthly Fee: $25 per month, plus applicable taxes.
Service Fees for Claims:a. $99 for PCs, Televisions, mesh Routers, Portable Gaming Devices, Printers, Home Theater Systems in a Box, Bluetooth and Wi-Fi Enabled Speakers, Virtual Reality Headsets, Pet Collars, Robotic Vacuums, Robotic Mops, or Augmented Reality Glasses.b. $49 for Audio Headsets, Health and Fitness Bands, Smartwatches, Smart Door Locks, Smart Home Security Cameras, Smart Thermostats, Smart Video Doorbells, Smart Video Products, Auto Fetch Machines, or Pet Snack and Food Dispensers.c. $0 service fee for the repair or replacement of remote controls, game controllers, monitors, keyboards, mouse, modems, external PC speakers, non-mesh Routers, certain smart alarm sensors, Audio/Video Streaming Devices, DVD Players, Smart Device Hubs, Smart Light Dimmers, Smart Smoke Detectors, Smart Carbon Monoxide Detectors, Blood Pressure Monitors, Pulse Oximeters, or EKG Monitors (though repair/replacement costs apply to the aggregate claim limit).
Installation Fee: $49 per product for home tech installations.a. Telco Home Internet customers (5G Home or Fios) enrolled in VHDP get $0 installation for eligible home tech.
4. Coverage Details
4.1. What's Covered:
An unlimited quantity of eligible products of any size and brand located at the Telco service address or a qualifying secondary address.
Categories & Examples:a. Home Entertainment and Office: Desktops, Laptops, Tablets (PCs, including one associated external monitor, keyboard, mouse, modem, external speakers), Home Routers (excluding Telco-branded/provided), External Hard Drives, Printers (excluding 3D/crafting), Televisions (LCD, Plasma, LED, including original remote).b. Other Home Entertainment: Bluetooth and Wi-Fi Enabled Speakers, Audio/Video Streaming Devices (excluding Telco-branded/provided), Gaming Systems, Handheld Gaming Devices (including controllers), DVD Players, Blu-ray Players, Home Theater Systems in a Box.c. Wearables: Health and Fitness Bands, Smartwatches, Virtual Reality Headsets, Audio Headsets, Augmented Reality Glasses.d. Smart Home Products: Hubs, light dimmers, thermostats, smoke/carbon monoxide detectors, door locks, security cameras, video doorbells, alarm sensors/keypads/panic buttons/range extenders, video products. These must use approved home networking solutions (Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, ZigBee, Z-Wave, etc.).e. Smart Home Healthcare Products: Blood Pressure Monitors, EKG Monitors (personal home use), Pulse Oximeters.f. Smart Pet Tech: Pet Collars (excluding fence hardware), Auto Fetch Machines, Pet Snack/Food Dispensers.g. Smart Floorcare: Robotic Vacuums, Robotic Mops.
Types of Damage/Issues:a. Mechanical or electrical failure due to defects in materials/workmanship, normal wear and tear, power surges.b. Unintentional and accidental damage from handling (ADH) for certain portable products (Laptops, Tablets, Portable Gaming Devices, Audio Headsets, Health and Fitness Bands, Smartwatches, Printer display screens, portable DVD/Blu-ray players, VR Headsets, A/V Streaming Devices, AR Glasses, Pet Collars, Blood Pressure Monitors, Pulse Oximeters, EKG Monitors).
Second Residence Coverage: Protects eligible home tech in a second residence of your choice at no additional cost. The secondary address can be changed up to three times per year. It must be used primarily for residential, not business, purposes. Claims at both addresses count towards the aggregate claim limit.
4.2. What's NOT Covered (Exclusions Summary):
Pre-existing conditions before enrollment or within the 30-day waiting period.
Cosmetic defects not affecting functionality (e.g., cracked cases, finish defects).
Breakdown from improper installation, unauthorized modifications/repairs.
Consequential or incidental damages (loss of use, data, profits).
Design flaws or systemic defects.
Damage from external physical force (unless ADH is specified for the product), acts of God, war, fire, liquid immersion (unless ADH is specified), misuse, negligence.
Installation/uninstallation costs (unless covered by a specific installation service).
Burned-in images, pixel failure within design specs.
Products not owned by you, leased/rented products, or those not customarily at your specified residence.
Damage during transit (except when at the designated repair depot).
Routine maintenance, cleaning, customer education.
Products with altered/removed serial numbers.
Products outside the United States.
Repairs covered by a manufacturer recall.
Software issues, viruses, loss of data.
Theft or loss of the product.
Custom-built products.
Consumable parts (batteries (except one annual laptop battery), bulbs, cartridges).
PCs without administrator permissions or inability to upload/download software.
Liability for property damage or injury/death from product use.
Telco-branded or provided routers and audio/video streaming devices (these are supported by Telco directly).
5. Key Benefits & Services
Repair or Replacement: If an eligible product experiences a covered breakdown, it will be repaired or replaced. If it can't be serviced, you may receive a replacement product, gift card, or check based on the product's value, age, and condition.
24/7 Tech Support (Tech Coach): Access to expert help via chat (Tech Coach app) or phone for troubleshooting, device tips, and self-help guides.
In-Home Expert Visits: Two in-home visits per 12-month period for help with product setup, configuration, Wi-Fi optimization, and network assessments. Does not include physical installations requiring tools.
Complete Tech Care: Includes tune-ups, cleaning, and recycling for laptops, tablets, and gaming consoles.
Home Tech Installations:a. $49 service fee per product for installation of items like TVs (wall-mounted), soundbars, smart thermostats, wireless network setups, smart hubs, smart doorbells, and Wi-Fi security cameras.b. $0 installation for eligible home tech for Telco Home Internet (5G Home or Fios) customers enrolled in VHDP.c. Available in select locations for select products; products must be new or fully functioning, and Wi-Fi access may be required.
6. Claims Process
How to File: Online at asurion.com/telcohomedeviceprotect or by calling 844-769-1991, 24/7.
Waiting Period: 30-day waiting period from enrollment before a claim can be filed. Technical support, in-home visits, and installations are available from the date of enrollment.
Claim Limits:a. Per Claim Limit: $3,000.b. Aggregate Annual Claim Limit: $5,000 in any 12-month rolling period (starts from the date of the first claim).c. Unlimited number of claims can be filed as long as they are within these monetary limits.
Reporting Deadline: Claims must be reported within 180 days of the product breakdown.
Claim Tracking: Via email updates or on Asurion's website / by phone.
7. Plan Management
Enrollment: Via the Services & perks page in My Telco (website or app).
Cancellation: Can be cancelled at any time online, in the My Telco app, by phone, or in a Telco store. A prorated refund of the monthly fee will be given.a. If cancelled after the first 30 days, coverage continues for an additional 30 days, and claims can be filed within 60 days of the cancellation date.
Second Residence Management: Add or change the second covered address via the Asurion dashboard (my.asurion.com/dashboard) or when filing a claim.
8. Contact Information
For Claims & Service:a. Online: asurion.com/telcohomedeviceprotectb. Phone: 844-769-1991
For Technical Support via Tech Coach:a. Through the Tech Coach App.b. Phone (say "Telco Home Device Protect"): 800-922-0204
To Track Aggregate Claim Limit: Call Asurion at 844-409-1998.
Important Note: This knowledge base is a summary. For complete details, including all terms, conditions, limitations, and exclusions, refer to the official Telco Home Device Protect Terms and Conditions documents.
Telco LTE Home Internet
Telco LTE Home Internet
Telco LTE Home Internet is a new Home Internet solution that uses Telco’s 4G LTE cellular network to provide fast and secure broadband connectivity to your home. It’s affordable, widely available and easy to set up, and enough to support multiple devices and everyday online activities. 
Currently there are 2 tiers of LTE Home Internet: Telco LTE Home (which includes download speeds of up to 50 Mbps and typical upload speeds of 4-5 Mbps) and Telco LTE Home Plus (which includes typical download speeds of 25-50 Mbps and typical upload speeds of 4-5 Mbps). (Note that currently for every billing cycle, for the first 150 GB of data, users will have 10-25 Mbps download speeds, and 2-6 Mbps upload speeds. After 150 GB of data used for every billing cycle, users will have 3-10 Mbps of download speeds, and 1-3 Mbps of upload speeds.)
To check availability in your area, enrollment information and more about this service, visit: https://www.telco.com/home/internet/lte/#check-availability.
To view FAQs about this service, visit: https://www.telco.com/home/internet/lte/faq/. 
For account management and payment, please visit: [My Telco](https://m.telco.com/wMM0jUjn).
On the Telco Home App, if the user has LTE Home Internet, users can select Devices > Data usage, they can see a breakdown of their internet usage, in categories or by device. This page will also show users their current plan, how much data they have used in their current billing cycle, and how many days remaining in their current billing cycle.
Login
Login
Resolutions for login issues vary depending on the specific problem the user is experiencing.
Here are the resolutions for different login-related problems:
For users unable to access their account, link mobile and home accounts, or who are told their credentials are incorrect:
It is suggested they set up a "One Telco ID" to merge their accounts.
Users should be prompted to reset their password through the "Forget your info?" option.
If these steps fail, the user should be directed to contact customer service.
The last solution is to direct them to contact customer service by phone [Call us] (tel://+18009220204).
For users who report having to change their password continuously:
The recommended solution is to direct them to contact customer service by phone at [Call us] (tel://+18009220204).
Minimum Age Requirement
For the Telco Home app, effective January 1, 2026, due to new data privacy laws, this app is restricted to those who meet the minimum age requirements of 18 years of age to be able to use this app.
One Telco ID
2One Telco ID (91.00)
A One Telco ID allows you to manage all your Telco accounts (mobile and Fios) with a single login.
To setup One Telco ID, go to this link: https://www.telco.com/support/one-telco-id-video/
The purpose of One Telco ID is to solve recurring login issues, and to simplify account management by allowing you to use a single login to access and manage both your Telco mobile and Fios accounts. This is to solve the consistent problem of users being unable to log in to their Telco accounts. This eliminates the need for separate logins, making it easier to view and manage all your Telco services in one place.
In the case where users have logged in but not seeing the screen populated, the user can try to fix this by first making sure that they are not using any third-party equipment and also using the correct login ID (note that users may have multiple login credentials for their Telco Home Internet service and Telco Wireless service, or other Telco services), then restarting the app, making sure the app is up to date, and restarting their router, as these could be possible fixes.
If the user has multiple addresses that use Telco Home Internet services, and do not see the option to swap between addresses on the Home Page (01.00), then they may need to set up One Telco ID, as their other addresses may be controlled by different accounts.
