12.00.title: Digital Secure Home
12.00.body:
Digital Secure Home is a Telco service that protects devices connected to an eligible Fios router, that safeguards your router-connected devices from online threats and vulnerabilities.
There are 2 tiers for Digital Secure Home. The first tier is called Digital Secure Home, which is a basic tier that includes Malicious Website Protection and Vulnerability Assessment. The second paid tier is called Digital Secure Home Plus, which is an always-on 24/7 threat protection in addition to the basic tier features.
For Digital Secure Home Plus, the user can enroll for $5.99/mo, and features include: automatic and unlimited Vulnerability Assessment, Malicious Website Protection, sensitive data protection, detects abnormal device behavior, protects your devices from being compromised by brute force attacks, protects from Botnet attacks, and prevents attackers from exploiting vulnerabilities. 
Note that both tiers are not antivirus software solutions, and only protects devices connected to the router.
A vulnerability is a weakness or flaw in a device that can be exploited to perform unauthorized actions such as compromising its confidentiality, integrity or availability.
Note that Digital Secure Home and all of its sub-features and pages are only available to users who have compatible equipment (G3100, CR1000A or CR1000B), and are subscribed to the Digital Secure Home service.
Also note that Digital Secure Home is specifically for software protection, and is not the same as Telco Home Device Protect, which is a different feature in the app. 
Enrollment:
For the basic tier of Digital Secure Home, the user can enroll for $0/mo, and features include: Customer initiated monthly Vulnerability Assessment, Malicious Website Protection, and sensitive data protection. 
To enroll, users need to have compatible equipment first. Then users should look for the Digital Secure Home section on the home page, and follow the onscreen instructions to proceed with the enrollment process. (Note that if users have had this subscription but already cancelled, and are looking to subscribe again, they should go to the Discover page (05.00) instead.)
Upgrading, downgrading, cancellation:
Select the Subscription button on the Digital Secure Home page (12.04), and follow the onscreen instructions to manage their plan.

12.04.title: Digital Secure Home page
12.04.body: On the Digital Secure Home page (12.04), users can view the Threats Blocked (each threat can be unblocked manually), view the Vulnerability Assessment (where users can scan the network and all devices connected to the network for vulnerabilities, note that if the user has Digital Secure Home (basic), then the user will have a limit of 1 scan per month. ), manage their exceptions list (a list of URLs, IP addresses and domains that the user has chosen to be allowed and visited), and manage their Subscription to Digital Secure Home, and view the Support page (the support page will link out to an FAQ page: https://www.telco.com/support/residential/internet/essentials/fios-digital-secure-home-plus/).

13.00.title: Parental Controls profiles page
steps: Select the "Devices" tile from the Home page.
Tap the device you want to manage.
Select "Parental Controls" to set content filters, time limits, or pause internet access.
13.00.body:
The Parental Controls profiles page is the page where users can view, create and manage their Parental Controls profiles. 
At the top of the Parental Controls profiles page, is a title that indicates the number of profiles the user has created. To the right of this title is a text-link button called “Add profiles”, when selected, takes the user to the Create profile page (13.02).
Beneath the “Profile name’s profiles” title is a profile section for each of the Parental Controls profiles the user has created. Each profile section includes the name of the profile, how many devices are in the profile, an interactable device tile for each device in that profile, and a button called “Manage”, which, when selected, takes the user to the Profile details page (13.03) for that specific profile. 
Within each of the interactable device tiles of the profile section is the device nickname, the product name, and its status (Online (green), Weak Signal (orange), Offline (black)). When any of the interactable device tiles are selected, will take the user to the Device details page (04.02).
At the bottom of the page is a button called “Cancel Parental Controls”, when selected, takes the user to cancel their Parental Controls subscription, where the user can follow the onscreen instructions to do so.
Assign Devices Page

13.01.title: Assign devices page
13.01.body:
The Assign devices page (13.01) displays multiple device lists for the user. There are two variations of this page, depending on how the user accessed it.
At the top of the Assign devices page, is the header section. 
New Profile, if the user is coming from the Create Profile Page (13.02):
The first list only shows unassigned devices, devices that currently are not assigned to any profile. The second list, shows devices that are part of other profiles, allowing the user to reassign devices.
There are two buttons at the bottom of the page: “Next”, and “Cancel”. Selecting “Next” will take the user to a page where they can select their preferences for Filtering content, and after that is done, then the profile will be successfully created. Selecting “Cancel” will also return the user to the previous, but will not create the device group.
Existing Profile, if the user is coming from Profile name’s devices page (13.05):
The first list shows the devices currently associated with the profile the user is viewing. The second only shows unassigned devices, devices that currently are not assigned to any profile. The third list, shows devices that are part of other profiles, allowing the user to reassign devices from other profiles.
There is one button at the bottom of the page: “Done”. Selecting “Done” will assign the selected devices and will also return the user to the Profile name’s devices page
A single Parental Control profile can hold up to 16 devices. There are no limits to the number of profiles that can be set up.
A device can only be assigned to one profile at a time.
Create Profile Page

13.02.title: Create profile page
13.02.body:
The Parental Controls "Create Profile" page enables users to group their children's devices for managing internet access. 
At the top of the Create profile page, is an “Add photo” button. Selecting this button will prompt the user to take a photo, or select a photo from their library. This will display as the photo for this profile. This step is optional. (Note that the image needs to be in either JPG or PNG, and needs to be smaller than 15 MB).
Under the photo section is the step to “Name this profile”. This is a required field.
Below that is “Select a color”. This allows the user to assign different colors for their children's profiles.
Below color selection are two buttons, “Next” and “Cancel”. Selecting “Next” will bring the user to an Assign devices page (13.01). Selecting “Cancel” will return the user to the previous page.
There is no limit to the number of profiles a user can set up.
Note that if coming from the Profile details page (13.03), this page will instead have a “Save” button and a “Delete profile” button at the bottom (there will not be Next or Cancel buttons in this case), selecting the Save button will save any updates made to the profile and takes the user back to the Profile details page (13.03). Selecting the Delete profile button will delete this current profile, and all devices assigned to this profile will become unassigned.
Edit Profile Page

13.03.title: Profile details page
13.03.body:
The Profile details page allows the user to manage a Parental Controls profile. 
At the top of the Profile details page, is the profile picture for this profile. Below that is the name of the profile. Below that are 2 buttons: “Pause Internet” and “Bonus Time” (note that the Bonus Time button will only show when the user has already set up a Daily Limit). The “Pause Internet” button, when selected, will pause the internet connection on all devices associated with this profile. If the profile is paused, this button will instead say “Unpause”. The “Unpause” button, when selected, will unpause the internet for this profile. The “Bonus Time” button, when selected, will allow the user to extend the current day’s Daily Limit. On the top-right corner of this section, is an “Edit” button, which will bring the user to the Create profile page (13.02). (Note that this Pause feature blocks new online activity and newly loaded content from accessing the internet. However, content that is already downloaded or buffered may still work.)
Beneath the “Pause Internet” button is a large interactable Usage tile that shows: the amount of time that the devices in this profile has been online today, a progress bar that indicates the time online today in relation to the Daily Limit for this profile for this day (the Daily Limit is set in the Daily Limits page (13.07)), the Daily Limit for this profile, and a CTA that says “View usage”, when this tile is selected, takes the user to the Usage history page (13.06).
Below the Usage tile is a section called “Settings & controls”. Within the Settings & controls section are 4 tiles.
The first tile is called Devices, with an indicator that indicates how many devices are assigned to the current profile, when selected, takes the user to the Profile name’s devices page (13.05). The second tile is called Filter Content, with an indicator that indicates which content filter this profile is currently using, when selected, takes the user to the Filter Content page (13.04). The third tile is called Daily Limit, with an indicator of what the user has set as the Daily Limit for this profile, when selected, takes the user to the Daily Limit page (13.07). The fourth tile is called Set Schedules, with an indicator showing how many schedules the user has set for this profile for today, when selected, takes the user to the Set Schedules page (13.08) (setting a schedule will allow the user to restrict internet access for this profile based on a recurring schedule).
Filter Content page

13.04.title: Filter Content page
steps: Open Parental Controls and select the profile.
Open Filter Content.
Adjust the age group, privacy separator, safe search, or website filters.
Save the content-filter settings.
13.04.body:
The Filter Content page is the page where users can filter content by age range for their Parental Controls profiles. 
At the top of the Filter Content page, is a tile called “Age group”, and indicates the current selected age group filter, if none is selected, will say none, and when selected, will allow the user to select an age group filter. 
The selectable filters include none, young child (ages 5-7), child (ages 8-12), and teen (13-17).
Beneath the Age group tile is a section called Privacy and safety. This section includes 2 toggleable categories to block: Google Safe Search and YouTube Restricted Mode, both of these are determined by Google and YouTube Respectively. 
Beneath the Privacy and safety section is the Categories & apps section, where within each category contains apps and websites pertaining to that category and can be turned on (blocked) or off (unblocked) by the user. These categories include Objectionable, Social media, Messaging, Games, Video, Music, Dating, Email, More and Individual websites. 
In this section the user can also search for a specific app to be blocked.
Within the Individual websites category, the user can manually enter an URL to be blocked, or the user can remove them from the list.
Note that if the user selects one of the Age group filters, then some of the apps and websites within certain categories are automatically turned on (blocked).
Note that this feature blocks new online activity and newly loaded content from accessing the internet. However, and content that is already downloaded or buffered may still work.
Profile name’s devices page

13.05.title: Profile name’s devices page
13.05.body:
The Profile name’s devices page is the page where users can see the devices that are currently assigned to the current profile. (Note that the page name and page header will change, where “Profile name” will instead be the name of the current profile’s name, for example, if the profile is named “Casey”, then the page and page header will be called Casey’s devices). To reach this page, navigate to the Parental Controls section on the home page. From there, select "All profiles" to go to the Parental Controls profile page, and then select "Manage"  on the Profile that you want to see the details.Select on devices tile under settings and controls to access this profile’s assigned devices.
At the top of the Profile name’s devices page, is a button that says “Add or remove devices”, when selected, will take the user to the Assign devices page (13.01).
Beneath the button is a list of the currently assigned devices for this profile.
Usage history page

13.06.title: Usage history page
13.06.body:
The Usage history page is the page where users can see the usage history of all devices currently assigned to this profile. 
At the top of the Usage history page, is an indicator of how much time the devices assigned to this profile has spent online within a certain category of time (Today, Yesterday, Last 7 days, Last 30 days, in tabs that the user can switch to see the respective time frames that the assigned devices for this profile has spent online), a graph of the currently selected time frame tab and the amount of time this profile has spent online.
Daily Limit page

13.07.title: Daily Limit page
13.07.body:
The Daily Limit page is the page where users can see and manage the daily usage limits of all devices currently assigned to this profile. Daily Limit manages the amount of time the user’s child spends online each day. 
At the top of the Daily Limit page, is the header section.
If there are no Daily Limits set yet for this profile:
Beneath the header section is a button at the bottom of the page, called “Get started”, when selected, will take the user through setting up time frames of daily limits. The user can select days of the week, the time limit for the selected days.
Note that you can only include each day of the week once, for example, if the user has already set a 1 hour limit for Monday for a particular profile, then Monday will be unselectable when setting another Daily Limit for this profile.
If there are already Daily Limits set for this profile:
Beneath the header section is a section called Daily Limit. To the right of this section is a toggle button, and when toggled on, will enable all Daily Limits on this profile, and when toggled off, will disable all Daily Limits on this profile (meaning devices on this profile will have unlimited time online).
Within the Daily Limit section is a list of the Daily Limits set for this profile. Each item on the list will have the amount of time the devices assigned to this profile can be online, and the days of the week where this amount of time will be enforced.
Selecting any item on the list will allow the user to edit that Daily Limit time and day, or delete that Daily Limit list item, with a Delete Limit button on this edit screen.
Note that this feature blocks new online activity and newly loaded content from accessing the internet. However, and content that is already downloaded or buffered may still work.
Set Schedules page

13.08.title: Set Schedules page
aliases: set bedtime schedule for child internet
bypass time controls change mac address
steps: Open Parental Controls and select the profile.
Open Set Schedules.
Choose School Time, Focus Time, or Bedtime.
Set the days and times, then save the schedule.
13.08.body:
The Set Schedules page is the page where users can manage the schedules set for this profile. Setting schedules allows the user to select days of the week and times when content can be restricted, with the option of allowing specific apps or websites within these schedules. 
At the top of the Set Schedules page, are 3 separate lists for schedule categories. The first list is called School Time, the second list is called Focus Time, and the third list is called Bedtime. Each list will contain the list items within each list, each list item will be a schedule that is already created for that list. The final list item for each list will be a button that allows the user to add another schedule for that list (for example, the button for the School Time list will be called Add School Time). For each list item, there will also be a toggle button, when pressed, will turn on or off the set schedule. Also for each list item, the user can long swipe left to delete that set schedule.
Each list item, when selected, will take the user to edit that set schedule, where the user can follow the on screen instructions to set the schedule. The user can set the days of the week, the start time and end time, the blocked content/apps by category (Objectionable, social media, messaging, games, video, music, dating, and email), or individual apps or websites (URLs).
When Add School Time, Add Focus Time, or Add Bedtime is selected, will take the user to create a new schedule, and the user can set the same parameters as when they are editing a schedule. 
Note that this feature blocks new online activity and newly loaded content from accessing the internet. However, content that is already downloaded or buffered may still work.

13.09.title: Edit profile page
13.09.body:
The Parental Controls "Edit Profile" page enables users to group their children's devices for managing internet access. 
At the top of the Edit profile page, is an “Add photo” button. Selecting this button will prompt the user to take a photo, or select a photo from their library. This will display as the photo for this profile. This step is optional. (Note that the image needs to be in either JPG or PNG, and needs to be smaller than 15 MB).
Under the photo section is the step to “Name this profile”. This is a required field.
Below that is “Select a color”. This allows the user to assign different colors for their children's profiles.
Below color selection are two buttons, “Save” and “Delete”. Selecting the Save button will save any updates made to the profile and takes the user back to the Profile details page (13.03). Selecting the Delete profile button will delete this current profile, and all devices assigned to this profile will become unassigned.
